title: Minimum Removals to Balance Array
description:
You are given an integer array nums and an integer k.
An array is considered balanced if the value of its maximum element is at most k times the minimum element.
You may remove any number of elements from nums​​​​​​​ without making it empty.
Return the minimum number of elements to remove so that the remaining array is balanced.
Note: An array of size 1 is considered balanced as its maximum and minimum are equal, and the condition always holds true.

Constraints:
hints: Sort nums and use two pointers i and j so that the window's minimum is nums[i] and maximum is nums[j].
Expand j while nums[j] <= k * nums[i] to maximize the balanced window; answer = n - (j - i + 1).
